methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Naifo Technologies is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Gaëtan Sévérac, having raised €54.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 9 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage peers, comprising 8 private companies and 1 public equity (277810.KQ). Key comparables include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), and Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B).

Peer-median methodology was applied, reflecting the primary valuation signal available. The median valuation across the 9-company peer set is $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This method anchors the primary estimate given the comparable stage and sector alignment, particularly the Series C peer Hadrian at $1.6B and adjacent-stage players clustered in the $1.6B–$2.0B band.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B (low) to $1.8B (high), with $1.6B as the median. This reflects a tight consensus among same-stage robotics and manufacturing peers, though the wide peer range ($300M–$10.0B) signals material dispersion in the sector. Key valuation uncertainties include differentiation within the crowded robotics space and the company's specific unit economics relative to the cohort. A single methodological input limits conviction; additional data on revenue multiples or growth rates would strengthen the analysis.
